what_it_does: This orienteering terrain entry documents the cache-company data substrate: a Chroma/SQLite vector index that embeds corpora/claude-sessions for semantic session recall, and records its layout, dependencies, observed evidence, and current stale/corrupt operational state.
observations: 1.6G cache at /home/tim/.cache/company/substrate-claude-sessions containing chroma/ (1009M), substrate.db (98M), config.json, and a quarantined chroma.corrupt-backup-20260620/
config.json maps one vault claude-sessions to /home/tim/corpora/claude-sessions and uses an ollama-style OpenAI-compatible endpoint at http://127.0.0.1:8007/v1
embedding model is perplexity-ai/pplx-embed-context-v1-4b with chunking set to 600 target / 64 overlap / 1400 max tokens
the reindex timer company-claude-sessions-reindex is inactive, while the embed service company-embed-pplx.service on :8007 is active
the index is stale: chroma/ mtime 2026-06-22, substrate.db mtime 2026-06-20, against an intended 20-minute reindex cadence
a quarantined corrupt backup chroma.corrupt-backup-20260620 is present, matching a prior '35904=stale chroma' bug context
the entry has no git remote, status unconfirmed, and was created 2026-06-12 with last active 2026-06-22
standouts: The vector cache is stale even though its embedding service is running, because the scheduled reindex timer is inactive
A quarantined corrupt Chroma backup sits alongside the live store, signaling a recent data-integrity incident
The entry explicitly separates observed evidence from inferred state and flags operational divergence
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, register, aliases, tags, relations, and coverage metadata
Labels claims as Observed or Verified to distinguish evidence from inference
Uses wikilink-style cross-references such as [[corpora]] and [[company-systemd]]
Records negative operational state (inactive timer, stale data, corrupt backup) rather than hiding it
concerns: The index is stale relative to its intended live cadence
A corrupt backup remains in the same directory as the live store
The upstream reindex dependency is inactive while downstream embed service appears active
No git remote and status unconfirmed reduce traceability
notes: This entry documents a stale 1.6G semantic session-recall vector cache with an inactive reindex timer and a quarantined corrupt backup.
questions: Why is company-claude-sessions-reindex.timer inactive while company-embed-pplx.service is active?
What caused the chroma corruption that produced chroma.corrupt-backup-20260620?
How much of the 1.6G total is the quarantined backup versus the live store?
What data does substrate.db hold that chroma/ does not?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a live session-recall vector index kept fresh by a 20-minute reindex cadence; actual state is stale because the reindex timer is inactive, so it is not currently fulfilling its live purpose.
intention_signals: Provide the Company with semantic memory over past Claude sessions by embedding markdown corpora into a locally-served vector store and keeping that index fresh via a scheduled reindex.
novelty: It combines a quarantined corrupt-backup artifact with explicit stale-state annotation in the same terrain entry, and uses a local ollama-style endpoint to serve a Perplexity embedding model.
contribution: A precise, evidence-annotated record of a semantic session-recall cache, including its data layout, embedding endpoint, chunking parameters, upstream and downstream dependencies, and current operational staleness and corruption status.
summary_for_embedding: cache-company is a 1.6G Chroma/SQLite vector cache at /home/tim/.cache/company/substrate-claude-sessions that embeds /home/tim/corpora/claude-sessions via a local :8007 Perplexity embedding endpoint for semantic session recall.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to maintain a live meaning-based index of past Claude sessions so the Company could recall them by semantic similarity.
